reasoning: In February 2025, Chinese researchers discovered a novel bat coronavirus (HKU5-CoV-2) that can enter human cells in the lab, but it has not been detected in humans and was deemed "not a public health threat." As of late October 2025, there are no reports of ANY new coronavirus (other than ongoing SARS-CoV-2 variants) infecting humans or causing a major outbreak. For the WHO to declare a entirely new COVID pandemic between now and December 31, 2025, a novel coronavirus would have to make the jump to humans, spread globally, and reach pandemic-level severity within a span of just two months. Given the base rate of pandemics and the Complete lack of early warning signs of an outbreak as of late October, the probability of this happening before the resolution date is as close to zero as possible.
